words: place kept quite, see. And so everybody had lots of 'em, all of 'em had

speaker: CC
words: all the feed they wanted. Because there was a lot of 'em didn't get thrashed

speaker: CC
words: at all. That stock would eat that headed wheat. And it was good feed,

speaker: CC
words: pretty good feed. And they got by if they had any cattle, why they got by

speaker: CC
words: and stuff like that. But if it was trying times, I ain't kidding you a bit.

speaker: CC
words: whole year's work all just went to pieces. Just rained and rained

speaker: CC
words: and rained and kept raining. And my father's thrashed out, he hadA engine

words: and thrasher. AndNthrashed that much and started raining that night and

words: never quit. And by gosh, rfc quit raining and he thought it would dry out.

words: And dad told him, says,"It ain't no use." So he says,Joe Humphry was quite

words: a name that had the hay. And he started to pull out, he bogged right down.

words: Then he, when we had to take horses and pull the machine out of the orchard.

words: I remember that.

words: That was tough.

words: Yeah. No, they couldn't. They couldn't leave. They didn't have nothing to

words: leave with. They had to stay. They had this headed wheat and stuff. And it.

speaker: CC
words: when they thrashed it they couldn't sell it. And they did thrash it, why

speaker: CC
words: it just made chicken feed and stuff. And we shipped -ftiat, we sold a bunch

words: of ours, we didn't need it and we sold it, hauled it in and shipped it and

words: they got chicken feed. Got a little money otit of It and then they got

words: groceries. That's the way we got through that time. And we had a hard time

speaker: CC
words: of it, now there ain't no fooling. And all your work was gone. And that's

speaker: CC
words: the only year of failure that I know of. Of the Palouse country.And then

words: there was one year that the crops only made about twelve bushels per acre.

words: I forget what happened that year. But they got enough to live. By the time

speaker: CC
words: Then after that, they watched it careful. And that land grew up, price

words: grew up on it and you could buy, for 25 dollars an acre, you could buy

words: that good wheatland. I could have bought 80 acres there in one spot, right

speaker: CC
words: close for 17 dollars an acre.

words: it was 175 miles from  and back. Now figure how long it would

words: take us.

words: and let the stock feed. There was some good grass and stuff. And we'd stop

words: and we had about 75 head of horses. And we had to have a night herd. Two

words: of, dad and me took one part of the night , the after part. And these

words: in the distance. Must have been pretty well onto a half a mile. And dad, he

words: pulled down with that big army gun and he let right em. Boy, of course

speaker: CC
words: whiter we didn't see any more. Now I don't know he killed one of 'em or not.

words: know.Sprangs. What they call Sprangs.

words: Sprangs. Yeah. A lot of Sprangs here together. In this country. We got in

words: hear the bell, and knew where they was.

words: left, the two boys,.they took our bed and we got on the horses and went

speaker: CC
words: out and put in the rest of the night. Keeping watch. And that old bell _.

speaker: CC
words: would have found out who it was.They started to go on this ridge. They

speaker: CC
words: spied us, see. And heard her. And they were making a getaway. And one of

speaker: CC
words: them got over the top here and we were almost here.And we seen'em going.

speaker: CC
words: him or what or that we didn't or anything about it. We never wait to see.
